name: Business & Community Lenders (BCL) of Texas- Dallas
description_short: BCL of Texas is a community development financial institution offering small business loans up to $50K, SBA 504 financing, and business coaching across Austin, Dallas, and San Marcos.
description_long:
Business & Community Lenders of Texas (BCL) operates as a CDFI serving Texas entrepreneurs and homeowners through three physical locations in Austin, Dallas, and San Marcos. The organization was founded on the principle of making capital accessible to diverse and underserved business owners while building stronger communities through strategic lending and financial coaching.

BCL's core business lending services include small business loans up to $50,000, growing business loans of $50,000 and above, SBA 504 loan programs for real estate and equipment purchases, and participation in the Texas Small Business Diversity Fund. Beyond lending, they provide no-cost one-on-one business coaching, classes, and technical assistance. The organization also operates community loan centers, manages loan funds, and provides real estate development financing for community impact projects.

What distinguishes BCL is their explicit focus on community development and generational wealth-building. They partner with major financial institutions (IBC Bank, Bank of America) for mortgage lending and homeownership programs, operate the NeighborhoodLIFT program, and run specialized initiatives like the San Marcos ThinkBIG entrepreneurship program and South Dallas Fair Park Business Growth Series. Their NMLS license (#1114924) is consistent across all locations, indicating they're regulated mortgage lenders as well.

The main limitation is that BCL functions as a hybrid CDFI serving multiple purposes (homeownership, small business, community development), which means business lending may not be their exclusive focus. Their loan limits for small businesses ($50K ceiling) are modest compared to traditional SBA lenders. The website emphasizes coaching and relationship-building over quick funding, suggesting this is a slower, more educational process than emergency or short-term lending.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Offers SBA 504 loans for real estate and equipment—documented success with commercial projects like Widner Finishing
No-cost one-on-one business coaching and classes; not a loan-only lender
CDFI with explicit focus on diverse and underserved business owners in Texas
Operates Texas Small Business Diversity Fund specifically for minority-owned businesses
Three physical locations (Austin, Dallas, San Marcos) with dedicated staffing and phone support
Regulated as mortgage lender (NMLS #1114924) with bank partnerships for integrated financing
Provides real estate development and community impact lending beyond simple business loans
cons: Small business loan cap of $50,000 limits utility for growing businesses needing larger capital
Website emphasizes coaching and relationship-building, suggesting slower approval timelines than other business lenders
Dallas location has single phone line (214-688-7456); no dedicated Dallas landing page despite office presence
Hybrid model serving homeownership, entrepreneurship, and community development means business lending is not their sole focus
Limited public information on loan terms, interest rates, or approval criteria on website
best_for: First-time Texas business owners seeking both capital and mentorship with diverse ownership backgrounds
Small businesses needing equipment or real estate financing (SBA 504 eligible projects)
Entrepreneurs in South Dallas, Austin, or San Marcos seeking community-focused lenders
diagnosis: BCL of Texas is best for early-stage and small business owners in Texas who value mentorship and community-focused lending alongside capital access, particularly those from underrepresented backgrounds. The primary caveat is that their $50K ceiling on small business loans limits scalability, and their emphasis on coaching suggests this is a relationship-driven process rather than fast-approval lending.
services: SBA 504 loan program (real estate and equipment)
Texas Small Business Diversity Fund participation
South Dallas Fair Park Business Growth Series
Homeownership counseling and mortgage lending partnerships
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 400 S Zang Blvd Suite 1220, Dallas, TX 75208
